metadata.prompt_for_train:
Classify the contribution type of ALL 4 genomes. All four genomes are from the same broad domain and have intentionally similar evidence. Options: method, dataset, analysis, system, theory

[Genome G1]
  Mechanism: DMT approximates density operators of 1D systems and is combined with TEBD time evolution to simulate dynamics.
  Niche: Dynamics simulation of strongly thermalizing 1D quantum systems across near-equilibrium and far-from-equilibrium initial states.
  Observation: The method performs well for Gibbs states with spatially varying temperatures, quenches across phase transitions, and pure states.
  Claim: DMT combined with TEBD makes it possible to simulate the dynamics of strongly thermalizing 1D systems to arbitrary times.

[Genome G2]
  Mechanism: Survey Kepler brightness measurements of 42,000 Sun-like stars for periodic transit dimmings, then inject synthetic planet-caused dimmings to measure detectability and correct occurrence estimates.
  Niche: Estimating how common Earth-size planets in habitable zones are around Sun-like stars.
  Observation: 603 planets were found; 10 were Earth size in habitable zones, implying 22% of Sun-like stars harbor such planets.
  Claim: 22% of Sun-like stars harbor Earth-size planets orbiting in their habitable zones.

[Genome G3]
  Mechanism: First-order convex optimization using proximal splitting on a staggered-grid discretization of the dynamic optimal transport problem.
  Niche: Computing discretized dynamic optimal transport, especially the L2 optimal transport geodesic between distributions on a uniform spatial grid.
  Observation: A centered-grid instantiation recovers the initial Benamou–Brenier algorithm, while the framework also supports more general costs and Riemannian manifolds.
  Claim: Proximal splitting schemes can solve the large-scale convex optimization problem arising from staggered-grid discretization of dynamic optimal transport.

[Genome G4]
  Mechanism: A stable, well-tested open-source Python implementation of the affine-invariant ensemble sampler for Markov chain Monte Carlo.
  Niche: Markov chain Monte Carlo sampling for scientific parameter-space exploration, especially published astrophysics applications.
  Observation: The sampler shows excellent autocorrelation-time performance, needs far fewer hand-tuned parameters than traditional MCMC, and enables easy multicore use.
  Claim: emcee provides an affine-invariant ensemble MCMC implementation that outperforms traditional MCMC in tuning burden and autocorrelation-time efficiency while enabling easy parallel execution.

Return the structured JSON answer with key `multi_contrib_types`.

Classify the contribution type of ALL 4 genomes. All four genomes are from the same broad domain and have intentionally similar evidence. Options: method, dataset, analysis, system, theory

[Genome G1]
  Mechanism: Fast decoder for topological codes correcting Pauli errors, erasures, and combined error-erasure patterns in O(nα(n)) worst-case time.
  Niche: Extremely fast error correction for large-scale quantum computers using topological codes.
  Observation: Worst-case almost-linear complexity, optimal correction up to distance bounds, and 2d-toric thresholds of 9.9% with perfect syndrome measurements and 2.6% with faulty measurements.
  Claim: The decoder corrects Pauli/erasure errors in topological codes with worst-case complexity O(nα(n)) and optimal performance up to the stated error and loss distance bounds.

[Genome G2]
  Mechanism: Embed items into a transition space, modeling each user as a translation vector operating over item sequences to capture user-conditioned transitions.
  Niche: Personalized sequential behavior prediction, or next-item recommendation, involving third-order interactions among a user, previously visited items, and the next item.
  Observation: TransRec outperforms state-of-the-art methods across a wide spectrum of real-world datasets.
  Claim: TransRec provides more accurate large-scale sequential recommendation than state-of-the-art methods on real-world datasets.

[Genome G3]
  Mechanism: AmaLgam integrates version-history bug prediction, similar-report analysis, and structural source-code analysis to rank likely buggy files for a submitted bug report.
  Niche: Automatic bug localization: given a bug report, output a ranked list of source code files likely needing fixes.
  Observation: On four open-source projects and more than 3,000 bugs, AmaLgam improved MAP over Sisman and Kak, BugLocator, and BLUiR.
  Claim: AmaLgam localizes buggy files more accurately than history-aware localization, BugLocator, and BLUiR as measured by mean average precision.

[Genome G4]
  Mechanism: End-to-end deep learning speech recognition replacing hand-engineered pipelines with neural networks, accelerated by HPC and GPU Batch Dispatch for scalable online deployment.
  Niche: Automatic speech recognition for English and Mandarin Chinese, including diverse speech across noisy environments, accents, and languages.
  Observation: HPC-enabled iteration made experiments much faster, and the resulting system was competitive with human transcription on several standard benchmarks.
  Claim: An end-to-end neural speech recognition system can recognize both English and Mandarin speech competitively with human workers on standard datasets while serving users online at low latency.

Return the structured JSON answer with key `multi_contrib_types`.

[G2] On large architectures and datasets, PackNet shows stronger robustness to catastrophic forgetting and can pack three fine-grained tasks into ImageNet-trained VGG-16 with near separate-network accuracy

Classify the contribution type of ALL 4 genomes. All four genomes are from the same broad domain and have intentionally similar evidence. Options: method, dataset, analysis, system, theory

[Genome G1]
  Mechanism: Learn maximum-entropy energy-based policies with soft Q-learning, representing the optimal policy as a Boltzmann distribution and training an amortized Stein variational sampler.
  Niche: Learning expressive policies for reinforcement learning in continuous state and action spaces, evaluated on simulated swimming and walking robots.
  Observation: The algorithm improves exploration and enables compositional transfer of skills between tasks in simulated robot experiments.
  Claim: Soft Q-learning can learn expressive maximum-entropy energy-based policies for continuous control that improve exploration and support skill compositionality.

[Genome G2]
  Mechanism: Relational Graph Convolutional Networks operate on graph-structured data and are specialized for highly multi-relational knowledge bases.
  Niche: Knowledge base completion: link prediction for missing subject-predicate-object triples and entity classification for missing entity attributes.
  Observation: R-GCNs work as a stand-alone entity-classification model and improve DistMult link prediction by 29.8% on FB15k-237 over a decoder-only baseline.
  Claim: R-GCN encoders improve knowledge base completion on multi-relational graphs, including significantly boosting DistMult link prediction over decoder-only baselines.

[Genome G3]
  Mechanism: Extends Dec-POMDPs to Dec-POSMDPs and solves them using closed-loop belief space macro-actions that support asynchronous decentralized planning.
  Niche: Multi-robot planning and coordination in continuous spaces under partial observability and uncertainty.
  Observation: On a complex uncertain package-delivery task, the approach naturally represented multi-robot problems and produced high-quality large-scale solutions.
  Claim: A Dec-POSMDP planner using closed-loop belief space macro-actions can scale better than previous methods while producing high-quality decentralized multi-robot plans under uncertainty.

[Genome G4]
  Mechanism: Original particle-level studies of relationships, complementarity, and dependence among jet substructure observables across jet properties.
  Niche: Jet substructure discrimination for quark/gluon jets and boosted heavy-object tagging, including W bosons and top quarks.
  Observation: Observable relationships and complementarity are studied as dependent on underlying jet properties, especially jet radius and transverse momentum.
  Claim: Particle-level correlation studies can clarify which jet substructure observables are complementary for quark/gluon discrimination and boosted W/top tagging.

Classify the contribution type of ALL 4 genomes. All four genomes are from the same broad domain and have intentionally similar evidence. Options: method, dataset, analysis, system, theory

[Genome G1]
  Mechanism: Derives a simple formula for average fidelity between a unitary quantum gate and a general quantum operation on a qudit, with a simplified proof linking it to entanglement fidelity.
  Niche: Estimating average gate fidelity for quantum dynamical operations on qudits.
  Observation: The qudit formula generalizes the prior qubit formula and may support experimental determination of average gate fidelity.
  Claim: A simple formula can compute the average fidelity between a unitary quantum gate and a general quantum operation on a qudit.

[Genome G2]
  Mechanism: A Bayesian probabilistic poker model separates game-dynamics uncertainty from opponent-strategy uncertainty, infers posteriors over strategies, and selects responses to that distribution.
  Niche: Opponent modelling and response selection in poker games with non-deterministic dynamics, partial observability, and unknown adversaries.
  Observation: The approach is demonstrated on reduced poker with Dirichlet priors and full Texas hold'em with a more informed prior, yielding effective posterior-based responses.
  Claim: Bayesian posterior inference over opponent strategies enables effective response play in reduced poker and full Texas hold'em.

[Genome G3]
  Mechanism: Use K2 mission observations and Laplace-relation expectations to constrain TRAPPIST-1 h and place it within a three-body resonant chain.
  Niche: Orbital characterization of the seventh Earth-sized transiting planet in the ultracool-dwarf TRAPPIST-1 system.
  Observation: TRAPPIST-1 h has an 18.77-day orbital period, radius of 0.752 Earth radii, equilibrium temperature of 173 K, and participates in system-wide three-body resonances.
  Claim: TRAPPIST-1 h orbits every 18.77 days and is linked to the other six planets through three-body resonances.

[Genome G4]
  Mechanism: Generalize LSTM recurrent units from linear chains to tree-structured network topologies that compose words into phrases.
  Niche: Predicting semantic relatedness between sentence pairs and sentiment classification of natural-language sentences.
  Observation: Tree-LSTMs outperform existing systems and strong LSTM baselines on SemEval 2014 semantic relatedness and Stanford Sentiment Treebank sentiment classification.
  Claim: Tree-LSTMs outperform existing systems and strong LSTM baselines on sentence semantic relatedness and sentiment classification tasks.

Return the structured JSON answer with key `multi_contrib_types`.

Classify the contribution type of ALL 4 genomes. All four genomes are from the same broad domain and have intentionally similar evidence. Options: method, dataset, analysis, system, theory

[Genome G1]
  Mechanism: Distributed mini-batching converts serial gradient-based online prediction algorithms into distributed algorithms while accounting for inter-node communication latency.
  Niche: Web-scale online prediction and distributed stochastic optimization where input rates exceed what a single processor can handle.
  Observation: The method achieves optimal regret for smooth convex stochastic settings, asymptotically linear multi-processor speed-up, and works on a web-scale online prediction problem.
  Claim: Distributed mini-batching can transform many serial gradient-based online prediction algorithms into distributed algorithms with asymptotically optimal regret for smooth convex stochastic losses.

[Genome G2]
  Mechanism: Phase reduction and averaging applied to a Langevin approximation of a neural master equation with intrinsic and extrinsic noise.
  Niche: Stochastic phase synchronization in uncoupled neuronal population oscillators driven by common extrinsic noise and disrupted by intrinsic noise.
  Observation: Intrinsic independent noise and extrinsic common noise can combine to produce clustering of population oscillators through multiplicative noise effects.
  Claim: A neural master-equation population oscillator driven by common extrinsic noise will exhibit synchronization effects modulated or disrupted by intrinsic noise, and can form clusters under multiplicative noise.

[Genome G3]
  Mechanism: A graph CNN framework extracts locally connected regions from arbitrary graphs and learns feature representations over those regions.
  Niche: Learning from graph data, including undirected or directed graphs with discrete and continuous node and edge attributes.
  Observation: Learned graph feature representations are competitive with state-of-the-art graph kernels and are highly efficient to compute.
  Claim: A convolutional neural network framework can learn efficient, competitive feature representations for arbitrary graph data.

[Genome G4]
  Mechanism: Continuous-time quantum walk implemented efficiently in a black-box graph setting to traverse the graph rapidly.
  Niche: A black box graph traversal problem requiring traversal of a graph.
  Observation: The quantum walk rapidly traverses the graph, yielding exponential speedup over any classical algorithm.
  Claim: This black box graph traversal problem can be solved exponentially faster on a quantum computer than on a classical computer.

Return the structured JSON answer with key `multi_contrib_types`.

metadata.prompt_for_train:
Given a predecessor genome A and successor genome B, classify the PRIMARY DRIVER of the change and the evolutionary DYNAMICS.

[Genome A (predecessor)]
 Mechanism: Replace example-loss minimization requiring entity resolution with equivalent minimization of a different loss over Rademacher observations, many computable without matching.
 Niche: Learning a classifier over the cross-product feature space of distributed datasets describing the same entities when no shared identifier is available.
 Observation: Large subsets of rados can avoid entity matching, and optimizing rado loss can be faster and smaller in space than equivalent example-loss learning.
 Limitation: The model’s key assumption is vertical partitioning among peers; in more general settings, existence of an entity-resolution solution may be unknown.

[Genome B (successor)]
 Mechanism: A deep reinforcement learning autonomous-driving framework incorporating recurrent neural networks for partial observability and attention models to focus computation on relevant information.
 Niche: Autonomous driving, especially maneuvering under environmental interaction with other vehicles, pedestrians, roadworks, and partially observable road scenarios.
 Observation: In TORCS, the framework learned autonomous maneuvering on complex road curvatures with simple interactions involving other vehicles.
 Limitation: Evaluation is limited to simulation in TORCS and only simple interaction with other vehicles; automotive deep reinforcement learning remains a relatively new area.


Dynamics options: Mutation | Adaptive Radiation | Hybridization | Speciation | Niche Competition
Driver options: mechanism | niche | observation | limitation

[Genome A (predecessor)]
 Mechanism: A deep neural network sequentially predicts raw pixel values along both spatial dimensions, modeling their discrete probability and complete image dependencies with two-dimensional recurrent layers and residual connections.
 Niche: Unsupervised modeling of natural image distributions with an expressive, tractable, scalable generative image model.
 Observation: The model achieved substantially better log-likelihoods than prior state of the art, benchmarked on ImageNet, and generated crisp, varied, globally coherent samples.
 Limitation: 

[Genome B (successor)]
 Mechanism: Crowdsource dense image annotations of objects, attributes, pairwise relationships, region descriptions, and QA, then canonicalize entities and phrases to WordNet synsets for relationship-aware vision-language modeling.
 Niche: Image understanding for cognitive tasks such as image description and visual question answering requiring object interaction and relationship reasoning.
 Observation: Visual Genome contains over 108K images and is claimed as the densest and largest dataset spanning descriptions, objects, attributes, relationships, and QA pairs.
 Limitation: Existing cognitive vision models remain constrained by perceptual-task datasets that lack dense object-interaction annotations.

Classify the contribution type of ALL 4 genomes. All four genomes are from the same broad domain and have intentionally similar evidence. Options: method, dataset, analysis, system, theory

[Genome G1]
  Mechanism: Actor-critic adapted to condition on other agents’ action policies, plus ensemble-policy training for robustness.
  Niche: Deep reinforcement learning in mixed cooperative-competitive multi-agent environments requiring coordination among agent populations.
  Observation: Traditional single-agent methods struggle: Q-learning faces multi-agent non-stationarity and policy gradients suffer variance growth with more agents.
  Claim: An actor-critic method that considers other agents’ policies can learn complex multi-agent coordination and outperform existing methods in cooperative and competitive scenarios.

[Genome G2]
  Mechanism: Distort observed images by a small known shear, measure estimator response, and correct shear response, selection biases, and low-SNR correlated-noise bias.
  Niche: Accurate weak gravitational lensing shear measurement from available imaging data without galaxy priors or simulation-based calibration.
  Observation: Low signal-to-noise metacalibration introduces correlated noise causing significant bias, but the proposed formalism recovered input shear to better than 0.1%.

Classify the contribution type of ALL 4 genomes. All four genomes are from the same broad domain and have intentionally similar evidence. Options: method, dataset, analysis, system, theory

[Genome G1]
  Mechanism: Use unbalanced optimal transport as a fast, global similarity measure computed by a generalized Sinkhorn algorithm within LDDMM diffeomorphic registration.
  Niche: Diffeomorphic matching/registration of imaging data, including surfaces, volumetric images, fibre bundles, and synthetic or real datasets.
  Observation: Applying the optimal-transport similarity in LDDMM produced better matching results on synthetic and real data, fibre bundles, and surfaces.
  Claim: Unbalanced optimal transport similarity computed with generalized Sinkhorn improves diffeomorphic registration matching quality compared with standard similarity measures.

[Genome G2]
  Mechanism: Convert partial observations into a fully observed belief-space control problem, then apply dynamic programming to prove mean-field Nash equilibria and approximate finite-agent equilibria.
  Niche: Discrete-time partially observed mean-field games with infinite-horizon discounted costs and mean-field interactions among many decentralized agents.
  Observation: Mean-field equilibrium policies become approximate Nash equilibria when used by every agent in sufficiently large finite-player games.
  Claim: Under mild technical conditions, Nash equilibria exist for these partially observed mean-field games, and their equilibrium policies form approximate Nash equilibria in sufficiently large agent populations.

[Genome G3]
  Mechanism: Extend the doubly robust estimator from bandits to sequential reinforcement-learning off-policy value evaluation.
  Niche: Estimating the value of a new reinforcement-learning policy from data collected by a different policy.
  Observation: The estimator is empirically accurate on benchmark problems and useful as a subroutine for safe policy improvement.
  Claim: The sequential doubly robust estimator is unbiased and can have much lower variance than popular importance sampling estimators.

[Genome G4]
  Mechanism: Automatically learn inductive bias by sampling multiple related tasks and selecting a hypothesis space with good solutions across the task environment.
  Niche: Choosing a learner's hypothesis space for reliable generalization in environments of related machine-learning tasks.
  Observation: A hypothesis space that works across enough training tasks can generalize to novel same-environment tasks, with multitask learning potentially improving bounds.
  Claim: If a restricted hypothesis-space class contains a space that performs well on enough training tasks, that space will perform well on novel tasks from the same environment.

  Observation: The pipeline tiles about 250 square degrees and produces 24 kHz spectral resolution with 0.983 ms cadence for transient dispersed signals.
  Claim: A hybrid FFT/exact-phasing beamforming pipeline can survey ∼250 square degrees at CHIME’s full angular resolution with time-frequency resolution suitable for FRB detection.

[Genome G2]
  Mechanism: Discrete-measure approximation with a linear programming algorithm and, for Wasserstein barycenters, an efficient nonsmooth optimization algorithm.
  Niche: Equilibrium multi-population matching, especially Wasserstein barycenter problems in multi-marginal optimal transport with applications in image processing and statistics.
  Observation: Convergence of the discrete approximation is proved, and numerical results illustrate algorithmic efficiency.
  Claim: The proposed discrete approximation algorithms converge and efficiently solve Wasserstein barycenter instances.

[Genome G3]
  Mechanism: Online pulsar-candidate selection using a purpose-built tree-based machine-learning classifier, GH-VFDT, plus survey-independent candidate features optimized to separate noise from probable astrophysical signals.
  Niche: Real-time classification and prioritization of rapidly growing pulsar survey candidate streams and data volumes, especially for large surveys such as SKA and LOTAAS.
  Observation: Existing candidate-limiting filters can harm pulsar-search success, while the new feature-and-classifier approach processes about one million candidates every 15 seconds with 90%+ recall.
  Claim: The GH-VFDT feature-based online classifier can process millions of pulsar candidates in seconds while maintaining high pulsar recall and supporting discovery in modern surveys.

[Genome G4]

content:
Evidence indicates all four genomes contribute algorithms, metrics, or approaches rather than datasets, standalone systems, analyses, or theories:

- G1: “A machine-learning metric using dimensionality reduction and k-nearest neighbors”
- G2: “Learn a reward signal from non-expert human preference comparisons”
- G3: “provides correct algorithms”
- G4: “A Random Forest detection/classification algorithm”

```json
{
  "multi_contrib_types": {
    "G1": "method",
    "G2": "method",
    "G3": "method",
    "G4": "method"
  }
}
```

  Observation: The approach solved complex RL tasks with human feedback on less than one percent of agent-environment interactions and could train novel behaviors with about an hour of human time.
  Claim: RL agents can solve complex tasks without access to reward functions by learning from sparse non-expert human preferences over trajectory segment pairs.

[Genome G1]
  Mechanism: A monotone degenerate-elliptic Cartesian-grid Monge-Ampere discretization combining Wide Stencil simplicity with power-diagram-inspired optimal-transport accuracy improvements.
  Niche: Discretizing and solving the 3D Monge-Ampere operator on domains represented by Cartesian grids.
  Observation: Three-dimensional numerical experiments indicate the scheme is efficient.
  Claim: The proposed monotone Cartesian-grid discretization converges globally under a damped Newton solver and efficiently solves 3D Monge-Ampere problems when the Hessian condition number is uniformly bounded.

[Genome G2]
  Mechanism: Convex unbalanced optimal transport distances using equivalent dynamic geodesic and static Kantorovich coupling formulations for transport, creation, and destruction of mass.
  Niche: Defining optimal-transport-like distances between arbitrary positive Radon measures with unequal total mass.
  Observation: The Wasserstein-Fisher-Rao metric fits this class, gaining a static Kantorovich formulation and enabling curvature and Monge-problem formulations via Riemannian submersion.
  Claim: A class of unbalanced optimal-transport distances admits equivalent convex dynamic and Kantorovich formulations, including Wasserstein-Fisher-Rao as a special case.

[Genome G3]
  Mechanism: Builds from Euclidean gradient flows to metric-space theory, then Wasserstein optimal transport, geodesic convexity, and the Jordan–Kinderlehrer–Otto scheme for convergence.
  Niche: Theory of gradient flows, especially PDEs interpretable as Wasserstein gradient flows on probability measures.
  Observation: Some PDEs can be understood as gradient flows under the Wasserstein metric induced by optimal transport.
  Claim: Certain PDEs are gradient flows for the Wasserstein metric on probability measures, and the JKO scheme converges for the easiest cases.

[Genome G4]
  Mechanism: Reparameterize neural-network weight vectors to decouple their length from their direction, improving optimization conditioning.
  Niche: Training deep neural networks across supervised image recognition, generative modelling, deep reinforcement learning, recurrent models such as LSTMs, and noise-sensitive applications.
  Observation: The method speeds stochastic-gradient convergence, provides much of batch normalization’s speed-up, and has lower computational overhead.
  Claim: Weight normalization accelerates training of deep neural networks by improving optimization conditioning without introducing minibatch dependencies.

metadata.prompt_for_train:
Classify the contribution type of ALL 4 genomes. All four genomes are from the same broad domain and have intentionally similar evidence. Options: method, dataset, analysis, system, theory

[Genome G1]
  Mechanism: Kernel-discretized Lagrangian mesh-free hydrodynamics using high-order matrix gradients and Riemann solvers over volume overlap, implemented as conservative, second-order moving fluid elements.
  Niche: Astrophysical hydrodynamic simulations requiring adaptive, conservative treatment of self-gravity, cosmology, shocks, mixing instabilities, discs, and subsonic flows.
  Observation: Across many tests, the methods are competitive with moving meshes, outperform SPH and fixed grids on adaptivity, mixing, angular momentum, advection, and long-lived gaseous disc orbits.
  Claim: Mesh-free Lagrangian finite-volume methods in GIZMO will solve standard astrophysical hydrodynamics tests with SPH-like adaptivity and grid-like convergence while improving angular-momentum conservation.

[Genome G2]
  Mechanism: Derive planet radius distributions by spectral type, then convert occurrence into heavy-element mass inventories using exoplanet mass-radius/heavy-element relations, assuming spectral-type independence.
  Niche: Host-star-mass trends in Kepler planet populations and short-orbit exoplanet heavy-element budgets across F, G, K, and M stars.
  Observation: M dwarfs have more small but fewer Neptune-sized planets; average short-orbit heavy-element mass rises from F to M stars, roughly inversely with stellar mass.
  Claim: At Kepler-complete short periods and radii, lower-mass stars host a higher average heavy-element mass in exoplanets, requiring more efficient inward migration of planetary building blocks than current models provide.

[Genome G3]
  Mechanism: Adapt product quantization to store fastText word embeddings while avoiding quantization artifacts, yielding a compact text classifier.
  Niche: Text classification models that must fit within limited memory.
  Observation: Across benchmarks, the compressed model typically uses two orders of magnitude less memory than fastText with only slight accuracy loss.
  Claim: A product-quantized fastText-derived classifier can reduce memory by about two orders of magnitude while preserving nearly the same classification accuracy.

[Genome G4]
  Mechanism: Numerical simulations varying noise model, measurement circuits, and decoding algorithm to estimate surface-code fault-tolerant thresholds.
  Niche: Fault-tolerant quantum computation using the surface code with nearest-neighbor gates in two spatial dimensions.
  Observation: Estimated per-gate thresholds range from 0.502(1)% to 1.140(1)%, generally below previous estimates.
  Claim: For the simulated surface-code settings, the fault-tolerant threshold lies between 0.502(1)% and 1.140(1)% per gate.

[Genome A (predecessor)]

 Observation: Quadrotor experiments indicate fast, automatic, safe controller optimization without human intervention.
 Limitation: Requires an initial, low-performance controller and a safe performance threshold to determine which parameter settings may be explored.

[Genome B (successor)]

 Observation: On real-world data, the estimator was accurate across settings and competitive when used inside a learning-to-rank task.
 Limitation: Unbiasedness depends on derived conditions; when those conditions are violated, the paper reports reduced bias experimentally rather than an unconditional guarantee.

metadata.prompt_for_train:

[Genome A (predecessor)]
 Mechanism: Learn a deep recurrent validator that estimates whether a partial sequence can begin a full valid sequence, using a Bayesian active learning-inspired approach.
 Niche: Validity assessment for discrete objects represented as sequences, especially when discrete generative deep learning models produce invalid sequences.
 Observation: On a synthetic dataset, the validator can distinguish valid and invalid sequences.
 Limitation: The work is a step toward generative models that faithfully produce valid discrete objects, with validation demonstrated only on a synthetic dataset.

[Genome B (successor)]
 Mechanism: Planet Hunters searched Kepler Q0–Q17 archival transits, estimated single-transit periods from transit duration and stellar properties, and used follow-up imaging/spectroscopy to validate and characterize host stars.
 Niche: Long-period exoplanet candidates at orbital distances larger than 1 AU in Kepler systems, especially candidates with only one or two visible transits.
 Observation: The study found 41 candidates in 38 systems, with 75% at 1–3 AU, validated 7 planets, and observed frequent long-period transit timing variations up to 41 hr.
 Limitation: Single-transit orbital periods are estimated, close stellar companions can create blends, and dynamically interacting components require follow-up radial velocity and transit observations.

 Observation: Robust optimization unifies prior work and enables networks with significantly improved resistance to a wide range of adversarial attacks.
 Limitation: Robustness against first-order adversaries is only a stepping stone toward fully resistant deep learning models.

metadata.prompt_for_train:
Given a predecessor genome A and successor genome B, classify the PRIMARY DRIVER of the change and the evolutionary DYNAMICS.

[Genome A (predecessor)]
 Mechanism: Hamilton–Jacobi reachability safety filter using approximate dynamics, Bayesian confidence refinement, and least-restrictive intervention around arbitrary learning-based controllers.
 Niche: Safe learning-based control for uncertain robotic systems operating in the physical world, especially safety-critical robots during online learning.
 Observation: A quadrotor learned by policy-gradient reinforcement learning without crashing and safely retracted from a strong external disturbance despite using a simple point-mass safety model.
 Limitation: Safety analysis depends on approximate system dynamics and was demonstrated with a simple point-mass model for the quadrotor.

[Genome B (successor)]
 Mechanism: Use Self Organising Maps to learn transit-shape structure of Kepler/K2 signals and classify or rank candidates as planets versus astrophysical false positives.
 Niche: Ranking planetary candidates for follow-up in Kepler and K2 planet-hunting surveys, separating true planet signals from astrophysical false positives.
 Observation: Transit-shape SOMs distinguished known planets from known false positives with 87.0% success and ran the KOI list in minutes on a typical laptop.
 Limitation: Follow-up telescope resources are limited; the abstract reports no explicit methodological weakness.

metadata.prompt_for_train:
Given a predecessor genome A and successor genome B, classify the PRIMARY DRIVER of the change and the evolutionary DYNAMICS.

[Genome A (predecessor)]
 Mechanism: Computationally efficient false-positive-probability calculation combining transit signal properties, stellar colors, follow-up observations, and population assumptions for field stars and multiple-star systems.
 Niche: Automated validation of transiting exoplanet candidates, especially Kepler candidates where many signals remain unconfirmed.
 Observation: Many Kepler planet signals can be validated with limited follow-up, typically only a spectrum and an adaptive optics image, while false positives can be reliably identified.
 Limitation: Validation still depends on follow-up data and informed population assumptions; the broader pressure is scaling application to all Kepler candidates.

[Genome B (successor)]
 Mechanism: Use simultaneous, independent randomization of variables across experiments to derive combinatorial upper and worst-case lower bounds for identifying causal relations.
 Niche: Determining all causal relations among N observed variables under causal DAG assumptions without latent variables, sample selection bias, or feedback cycles.
 Observation: Allowing multiple variables to be randomized can reduce required experiments to log2(N)+1 in the unrestricted case, versus N-1 when randomizing at most one variable.
 Limitation: Results rely on no latent variables, no sample selection bias, and no feedback cycles; the observed-subset-of-DAG case remains conjectural.

[Genome A (predecessor)]
 Mechanism: Use the linear-nature explanation to derive a simple, fast method for generating adversarial examples, then use those examples in adversarial training.
 Niche: Neural network vulnerability to adversarial examples: small worst-case input perturbations causing high-confidence misclassification.
 Observation: Adversarial perturbations generalize across architectures and training sets, and adversarial training reduces MNIST test error for a maxout network.
 Limitation: 

[Genome B (successor)]
 Mechanism: Adversarially evaluate SQuAD systems by automatically inserting distracting sentences into paragraphs without changing the correct answer or misleading humans.
 Niche: Evaluating whether reading comprehension systems on SQuAD truly understand language under distracting paragraph perturbations.
 Observation: Sixteen models’ average F1 fell from 75% to 36%; with ungrammatical adversarial word sequences, four models averaged 7%.
 Limitation: Standard accuracy may overstate progress; future models need more precise language understanding.

[Genome A (predecessor)]
 Mechanism: Introduce three adversarial-example attack algorithms tailored to three prior distance metrics, plus high-confidence transferability testing, to evaluate and break defensive distillation.
 Niche: Robustness evaluation of neural networks against adversarial examples, especially defenses intended for security-critical applications.
 Observation: Defensive distillation did not significantly improve robustness: the new attacks succeeded on distilled and undistilled networks with 100% probability.
 Limitation: Current defenses remain vulnerable; stronger future defenses need benchmark attacks that resist adversarial examples under multiple distance metrics and transferability tests.

[Genome B (successor)]
 Mechanism: Survey and comparison of approximate Gaussian convolution algorithms using finite impulse response filters, DFT/DCT convolution, box filters, and recursive filters, with explicit boundary handling.
 Niche: Efficient Gaussian convolution for signal and image processing algorithms.
 Observation: Fast approximations exist across multiple algorithm families, but boundary handling is often underdeveloped; numerical experiments compare their speed and quality.
 Limitation: Boundary handling is sometimes overlooked in original Gaussian convolution works.

metadata.prompt_for_train:
Given a predecessor genome A and successor genome B, classify the PRIMARY DRIVER of the change and the evolutionary DYNAMICS.

[Genome A (predecessor)]
 Mechanism: A recurrent sequence-to-sequence network maps character embeddings to mel-scale spectrograms, then a modified WaveNet vocoder synthesizes time-domain waveforms from those spectrograms.
 Niche: Natural speech synthesis directly from text.
 Observation: Tacotron 2 achieves MOS 4.53, comparable to 4.58 for professionally recorded speech, and mel-spectrogram conditioning reduces WaveNet architecture size.
 Limitation: 

[Genome B (successor)]
 Mechanism: Add recurrency to DQN by replacing the first post-convolutional fully connected layer with an LSTM, producing a Deep Recurrent Q-Network that integrates information through time.
 Niche: Atari reinforcement-learning control under partial observability, including single-frame inputs and flickering game screens where complete game state is not always visible.
 Observation: DRQN matches DQN on standard and flickering Atari, scales with improved observability, and degrades less than DQN when evaluated with partial observations after full-observation training.
 Limitation: Recurrency provides no systematic advantage when learning to play the game; its main benefit is adaptation when observation quality changes at evaluation time.

metadata.prompt_for_train:
Given a predecessor genome A and successor genome B, classify the PRIMARY DRIVER of the change and the evolutionary DYNAMICS.

[Genome A (predecessor)]
 Mechanism: A deep neural network maps natural language to SQL using policy-based reinforcement learning from query execution rewards and SQL-structure-based pruning.
 Niche: Translating natural language questions into corresponding SQL queries over relational database tables.
 Observation: On WikiSQL, Seq2SQL substantially outperforms a state-of-the-art semantic parser in both execution accuracy and logical form accuracy.
 Limitation: SQL queries contain unordered parts that are less suitable for optimization via cross-entropy loss.

[Genome B (successor)]
 Mechanism: Hybrid GPU beamforming: 256 North-South FFT-formed beams crossed with four East-West exact-phased beams, plus zero-padding and up-channelization.
 Niche: Searching for and localizing Fast Radio Bursts across CHIME’s large instantaneous field-of-view while maintaining full angular resolution.
 Observation: The pipeline tiles about 250 square degrees and produces 24 kHz spectral resolution with 0.983 ms cadence for transient dispersed signals.
 Limitation: Wideband chromatic beam alignment remains a pressure point, handled with a zero-padding approximation rather than exact frequency-independent alignment.

 Limitation: Gibbs runtime depends on N, β, Z, and ε; hitting-time estimation assumes a sparse stochastic matrix and depends on ε and Δ.

metadata.prompt_for_train:

[Genome A (predecessor)]

 Niche: Nonparametric prediction-region construction under unknown distributions, targeting valid finite-sample coverage without distributional or bandwidth assumptions.
 Observation: Simulations demonstrate the method’s theoretical properties, while explicit loss convergence rates are established under standard regularity conditions.
 Limitation: Practical implementation and bandwidth choice require approximations and data-driven bandwidth selection methods.

[Genome B (successor)]

 Niche: Optimization difficulty and local-minimum structure in training deep neural networks.
 Observation: Deep networks are non-convex but lack poor local minima; non-global critical points are saddle points, with bad saddles appearing in deeper models.
 Limitation: Bad saddle points create a future-work pressure, and the theory remains separated from practical deep-learning behavior.

metadata.prompt_for_train:

[Genome A (predecessor)]
 Mechanism: Model-based reinforcement learning using statistical dynamics models and Lyapunov stability verification to synthesize high-performance policies with provable stability certificates.
 Niche: Safe reinforcement learning for real-world safety-critical systems where unsafe exploration can damage or destabilize the system.
 Observation: The algorithm safely optimized a neural network policy on a simulated inverted pendulum without the pendulum ever falling down.
 Limitation: Safe data collection and expansion of the safe region require additional regularity assumptions in terms of a Gaussian process prior.

[Genome B (successor)]
 Mechanism: Wasserstein-like dynamical transport distances for vector-valued densities, replacing scalar mobility with a positive, concave mobility matrix within a gradient-flow variational framework.
 Niche: Systems of nonlinear parabolic evolution equations and degenerate drift-diffusion systems for vector-valued densities on R.
 Observation: The framework yields structural distance properties, identifies geodesically convex energies and contractive flows, generalizes McCann’s condition, and constructs weak solutions even without geodesic convexity.
 Limitation: Some underlying energy functions are not geodesically convex with respect to the new distance, requiring de Giorgi’s minimizing movement scheme.

metadata.prompt_for_train:

[Genome A (predecessor)]
 Mechanism: Use the Story Cloze Test: give a system a four-sentence story plus two possible endings, and score whether it chooses the correct ending.
 Niche: Story understanding and script learning for short narratives requiring selection of the correct story ending.
 Observation: Eight systems participated, using a variety of approaches; strong performance requires linking multiple semantic levels to commonsense knowledge.
 Limitation: Systems remain pressured to approach human performance by better connecting semantics with commonsense knowledge.

[Genome B (successor)]
 Mechanism: An asynchronous decentralized stochastic gradient descent algorithm that avoids centralized parameter servers while enabling parallel worker updates.
 Niche: Distributed machine learning in heterogeneous environments requiring communication-efficient training without centralized bottlenecks.
 Observation: On ImageNet ResNet-50 up to 128 GPUs, AD-PSGD matches AllReduce-SGD epoch convergence while making epochs 4–8× faster in shared HPC networks.
 Limitation: The motivating pressure is heterogeneous and congested distributed training, where synchronous methods slow down and parameter-server asynchronous methods bottleneck or converge worse.
